articles.title: Broadstone adds Tesla recycling project as build-to-suit pipeline nears $180M
articles.summary: Broadstone Net Lease announced its Q1 2026 results, reporting a 5.6% year-over-year AFFO growth and adding $30 million to its build-to-suit pipeline, now totaling nearly $180 million. A significant new development includes a $30.4 million Tesla battery recycling facility in Austin, Texas, expected to stabilize in Q4 2027. The company's diversified portfolio consists of 773 net leased commercial properties and it reaffirmed its 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share.

articles.title: Matthews International Reports Q2 Results, Maintains 2026 Outlook
articles.summary: Matthews International (MATW) reported mixed Q2 2026 results, with growth in Memorialization and Product Identification offsetting declines in Industrial Technologies. The company maintained its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EBITDA guidance of $180 million, bolstered by a favorable arbitration ruling with Tesla, progress in its Propelis joint venture, and strategic debt reduction and divestitures aimed at enhancing shareholder value.

articles.title: Tesla Semi finally begins mass production
articles.summary: Tesla has officially started high-volume production of its Semi electric truck at Gigafactory Nevada. The company announced the milestone on X, indicating that the first Semi has rolled off its new production line. Deliveries of the electric truck are anticipated to commence later this year.

The company announced the milestone on X, indicating that the first Semi has rolled off its new production line. Deliveries of the electric truck are anticipated to commence later this year.

articles.title: First Tesla Semi rolls off high volume production line
articles.summary: Tesla announced that its first Semi truck has rolled off a high-volume production line, indicating it is on schedule for large-scale manufacturing of several new products in 2026. The all-electric truck is designed for long-haul freight with a range of up to 500 miles on a single charge. Tesla also plans to begin volume production of its Cybercab robotaxi and Megapack 3 battery system in 2026, with significant capital spending focused on expanding factories for these initiatives.

articles.title: Broadstone Net Lease Announces First Quarter 2026 Results and Adds $30 million to its Committed Pipeline of Build-to-Suit Developments
articles.summary: Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) announced its Q1 2026 operating results, reporting a 5.6% year-over-year AFFO growth and a net income of $46.4 million. The company invested $171.9 million in acquisitions and build-to-suit developments, including adding $30 million to its committed pipeline, exemplified by a new Tesla battery recycling facility project. BNL also reaffirmed its 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2925 per share.
articles.text_to_analyze: Broadstone Net Lease Announces First Quarter 2026 Results and Adds $30 million to its Committed Pipeline of Build-to-Suit Developments. BNL also reaffirmed its 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2925 per share.

articles.title: TD Bank (NYSE: TD) issues auto-callable notes with ≥14.85% coupon
articles.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering market-linked, auto-callable notes (Senior Debt Securities, Series H) with a fixed monthly coupon of at least 14.85% per annum. The notes are tied to the lowest performing common stock of AMD, META, MU, and TSLA, with a 20% buffered downside. While offering a high coupon, investors face the risk of losing up to 80% of the face amount if the lowest performing stock falls significantly below 80% of its starting price, and payments are subject to the Bank's credit risk.

articles.title: Tesla sued after worker says he was struck by carbon steel pipe
articles.summary: A welder at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas, Juan Jesus Chavez, is suing Tesla Inc. and his employer, EFX Industrial, after being struck by a carbon steel pipe. The lawsuit claims negligence by both companies, citing unsafe working conditions, improper training, and inadequate safety equipment. Chavez is seeking over $1 million in damages for his injuries, which include a closed head injury and orthopedic issues.

articles.title: Intel Stock Surges to All-Time High on Foundry Revival and Strong CPU Demand
articles.summary: Intel's stock has reached a new all-time high of $94.10 per share, marking over 400% growth in a single year, driven by the revival of its Foundry business and robust CPU demand. The company's success is attributed to government investment, improved yields across its Intel 4, Intel 3, and 18A nodes, and significant customer wins like Tesla. Additionally, Intel is repurposing silicon dies that would otherwise be scrap, demonstrating strong product execution and demand for its CPUs.

articles.title: Is Free Tesla Supercharging for a Year a Good Deal?
articles.summary: Tesla is offering one free year of Supercharging with the purchase of a Model 3 Premium or Performance. The value of this offer varies significantly depending on location and charging habits, potentially saving drivers $450-$950. The article breaks down the calculation, considering average mileage and electricity rates, and notes that most savings will be realized by those who frequently use Superchargers over home charging.

articles.title: Gigafactory Texas injury lawsuit: Welder hit by pipe sues Tesla, contractor
articles.summary: A welder at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas is suing Tesla and his employer, EFX Industrial, for negligence after he was struck by a carbon steel pipe in March. Juan Jesus Chavez claims insufficient safety protocols and lack of training led to him sustaining a closed head injury and orthopedic injuries, seeking over $1 million in damages. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about safety at the plant, especially after Travis County recently withheld some of Tesla's tax breaks due to incomplete documentation on construction site safety.
